questions.question: Which of the following was a major criticism of the Green Revolution in India?
questions.option_1: It led to regional and crop imbalances.

questions.question: Which organisation’s support was crucial in India’s globalisation process post 1991 economic crisis?
Colonial policies significantly altered India’s trade structure. Which of the following best describes India’s foreign trade pattern at the time of independence?
questions.option_1: Export of raw materials and import of manufactured goods
questions.option_2: Export of finished goods and import of raw materials
questions.option_3: Export of capital goods and import of consumer goods
questions.option_4: Balanced trade between agriculture and industry

questions.question: The main objective of British economic policies in India was to:
What was the main objective of India’s First Five Year Plan (1951–56)?
The Human Development Index emphasises people-centric development. Who is credited with creating the HDI alongside Amartya Sen?
Which of the following is considered a major challenge to human development in India?
During the colonial period, the services sector accounted for only _____.
Which of the following sectors employed the largest share of India’s population before independence?
In which of the following years was the monetary policy framework moved towards flexible inflation targeting and a six member Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) constituted for setting the policy rate?
The government tracks the performance of eight core industries to monitor industrial activity. What is the approximate weight of the core industries in the Index of Industrial Production (IIP)?
Which of the following options best defines 'Sustainable Development'?
questions.option_3: Development that meets present needs without compromising future generations

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the impact of 1991 reforms on agricultural exports?
questions.option_1: Only wheat and rice were allowed for export.
questions.option_2: Export promotion was encouraged through de-regulation.
questions.option_3: Export restrictions were imposed to ensure food security.

questions.question: During 1950–1990, India mainly exported which type of goods?
Which of the following indicators is NOT directly included in the calculation of the Human Development Index (HDI)?
Which of the following sectors has been classified as infrastructure by the Government of India for improved financing access?
In the first phase of the Green Revolution (approximately mid 1960s upto mid 1970s), the use of HYV seeds was restricted to the more affluent states such as _________.
questions.option_1: Punjab, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu

questions.question: Which of the following sectors contributed significantly to India’s GDP growth after the 1991 reforms?
Why is air transport less popular for freight movement in India?
questions.option_1: It is expensive and ideal only for light, high-value goods.
questions.option_2: It is widely available in rural and remote villages.
questions.option_3: It requires minimal infrastructure for operation.
questions.option_4: It is slower and less efficient than water transport.

questions.question: In which year was the Industrial Policy Resolution that gave importance to the public sector adopted?
Which sector was the dominant contributor to India's GDP immediately after independence in 1947?
Which of the following is a private sector organisation in India?
Who among the following presented the First Five-Year Plan on July 9, 1951?
The spread of the Green Revolution technology enabled India to achieve self-sufficiency in __________.
Which of the following Five-Year Plans was based on the Harrod-Domar Model?
